title: Architecture
text: lightweight (66.5kb) C++ application : - Initializes WebView2 runtime. - Establishes Zero-Port IPC bridge using standard input/output (stdin/stdout). - Manages parent window, native menus, system tray. - Includes Anti-Zombie Watchdog ensures R processes cleaned UI closes.

title: Architecture
text: R6-based event loop : - Runs non-blocking service loop process desktop messages. - Manages Async Engine background task execution. - Handles high-level desktop operations (File Dialogs, Clipboard, Notifications).

title: Architecture
text: standard HTML/JS/CSS surface : - Communicates R via global window.rdesk object. - Pushes events R using rdesk.send(type, payload). - Listens R updates using rdesk.(type, callback).

title: Architecture
text: Unlike traditional R web frameworks, RDesk open network ports (httpuv, WebSockets). Security: firewall prompts local network exposure. Reliability: “port use” errors multi-instance launches. Performance: Native pipe communication faster reliable loopback network stack.

title: Architecture
text: run app$run(): 1. R spawns native launcher child process. 2. launcher opens Windows window starts Chromium-based WebView2. 3. R enters (private$.running) loop, servicing stdin/stdout pipes. 4. user closes window, launcher signals R exit, ensuring clean shutdown background worker nodes.

what: The three tiers of async in RDesk
title: Async & Background Tasks in RDesk
text: RDesk provides three levels async support. Start Tier 1. Move Tier 2 3 need something Tier 1 .

previous_headings: The three tiers of async in RDesk
title: Async & Background Tasks in RDesk
text: Wrap message handler async(). RDesk handles everything else. async() automatically: - Runs function background worker (non-blocking). - Shows loading overlay cancel button. - Reloads packages worker context. - Sends result back <type>_result. - Shows error toast something goes wrong.
code: app$on_message("calculate_total", async(function(payload) {   # This runs in a background worker   result <- sum(payload$values)   list(total = result) }, loading_message = "Calculating..."))

previous_headings: The three tiers of async in RDesk
title: Async & Background Tasks in RDesk
text: Use rdesk_async() need manual control UI state multiple progress updates:
code: app$on_message("long_job", function(payload) {   job_id <- rdesk_async(     task = function(x) {         # Perform multiple steps, each reporting progress        for(i in 1:5) {           Sys.sleep(1)           # Note: You can send progress manually or           # rely on the final on_done.         }        x * 2      },     args = list(x = 10),     on_done = function(res) {       # Hide overlay when task finishes       app$send("__loading__", list(active = FALSE))       app$send("job_finished", list(result = res))     }   )   # Show overlay with explicit message and job_id for cancellation   app$send("__loading__", list(     active = TRUE,      message = "Starting long job...",      job_id = job_id,     progress = 10 # Initial progress   )) })

previous_headings: The three tiers of async in RDesk
title: Async & Background Tasks in RDesk
text: Use mirai::mirai() directly need: - Custom daemon counts per task type. - Task chaining pipelines. - Integration mirai-aware packages.

what: One rule that applies to all three tiers
title: Async & Background Tasks in RDesk
text: Background workers completely isolated main R session. see variables, loaded data, functions unless pass explicitly args. limitation – makes code reproducible safe.

what: Load a CSV with a file dialog
title: Cookbook
code: app$on_message("load_file", function(payload) {   path <- app$dialog_open(     title   = "Open CSV",     filters = list("CSV files" = "*.csv")   )   if (is.null(path)) return(invisible(NULL))    df <- utils::read.csv(path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)   app$send("file_loaded", list(     rows     = nrow(df),     cols     = names(df),     filename = basename(path)   )) })

title: Cookbook
code: app$on_message("get_chart", async(function(payload) {   p <- ggplot2::ggplot(mtcars,          ggplot2::aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +        ggplot2::geom_point(size = 3) +        ggplot2::theme_minimal()    list(chart = rdesk_plot_to_base64(p)) }, app = app)) rdesk.on("get_chart_result", function(data) {   document.getElementById("chart").src =     "data:image/png;base64," + data.chart; });

title: Cookbook
code: app$toast("File saved successfully", type = "success") app$toast("Could not connect",       type = "error") app$toast("Update available",        type = "info")

what: Save a file with a dialog
title: Cookbook
code: app$on_message("export_csv", function(payload) {   path <- app$dialog_save(     title        = "Save CSV",     filters      = list("CSV files" = "*.csv"),     default_name = "export.csv"   )   if (is.null(path)) return(invisible(NULL))    write.csv(mtcars, path, row.names = FALSE)   app$toast(paste("Saved to", basename(path)), type = "success") })

what: Run a slow computation without freezing the UI
title: Cookbook
code: app$on_message("run_analysis", async(function(payload) {   result <- slow_function(payload$data)   list(     summary = as.list(summary(result)),     n       = length(result)   ) }, app = app, loading_message = "Analysing..."))

title: Cookbook
code: app$on_ready(function() {   app$set_menu(list(     File = list(       "Open..."   = function() app$send("load_file", list()),       "Export..." = function() app$send("export_csv", list()),       "---",       "Exit"      = app$quit     ),     View = list(       "Refresh"   = function() app$send("refresh", list()),       "Dark mode" = function() app$send("toggle_theme", list())     ),     Help = list(       "About"     = function() {         app$toast("MyApp v1.0.0 -- built with RDesk", type = "info")       }     )   )) })

title: Cookbook
text: Host plain text file version_url containing latest version string, e.g. 1.1.0. RDesk checks silently launch installs update newer version available.
code: # In app.R, before app$run() rdesk_auto_update(   current_version = "1.0.0",   version_url     = "https://yourserver.com/latest.txt",   download_url    = "https://yourserver.com/MyApp-setup.exe",   app             = app )

what: Send data when the app starts
title: Cookbook
code: app$on_ready(function() {   df <- load_initial_data()   app$send("data_ready", list(     rows = lapply(seq_len(nrow(df)), function(i) as.list(df[i,])),     cols = names(df),     n    = nrow(df)   )) })

title: Cookbook
code: app$on_message("risky_operation", async(function(payload) {   tryCatch({     result <- operation_that_might_fail(payload)     list(success = TRUE, data = result)   }, error = function(e) {     list(success = FALSE, error = e$message)   }) }, app = app)) rdesk.on("risky_operation_result", function(data) {   if (!data.success) {     showError(data.error);     return;   }   renderResult(data.data); });

what: Detect if running as a built app vs dev mode
title: Cookbook
code: if (rdesk_is_bundle()) {   # Running as distributed exe   data_path <- file.path(getwd(), "data", "config.json") } else {   # Running in development via source("app.R")   data_path <- file.path(app_dir, "data", "config.json") }

title: Getting Started with RDesk
text: Data analysts build powerful tools R, sharing non-technical users difficult. Shiny excellent web dashboards, requires server steady network connection. RDesk solves turning R code native Windows desktop application. runs standalone .exe zero network overhead server required.

title: Getting Started with RDesk
text: RDesk works ---box modern Windows systems. need R Rtools installed.

title: Getting Started with RDesk
text: fastest way build RDesk application using built-scaffolding tool. generates professional dashboard sidebar, KPI cards, asynchronous charting engine. Navigate MyDashboard folder run app.R see results.
code: library(RDesk)  # Create a professional dashboard in a new directory RDesk::rdesk_create_app("MyDashboard")

title: Getting Started with RDesk
text: core value RDesk ability turn R code standalone .exe runs machine without requiring pre-installed R version. First build note: initial build takes 3-5 minutes RDesk downloads bundles portable R runtime. Subsequent builds complete 30 seconds. resulting .zip file dist/ folder contains exactly need distribute tool. end-user simply unzips runs MyProfessionalApp.exe.
code: # Build a portable ZIP bundle RDesk::build_app(   app_dir  = "MyDashboard",   app_name = "MyProfessionalApp" )

title: Getting Started with RDesk
text: project uses renv, commit renv.lock project root. RDesk’s GitHub Actions workflows automatically restore lockfile building. Additionally, build_app() writes bundle-level renv.lock distributable renv active.

title: Getting Started with RDesk
text: Zero ports, zero firewall issues. Communication happens standard input/output using high-speed JSON protocol.

title: Getting Started with RDesk
text: Use async() wrapper run heavy R tasks without freezing UI:
code: app$on_message("heavy_task", async(function(msg) {   Sys.sleep(2) # Simulate work   list(status = "Done") }, app = app))

title: Getting Started with RDesk
text: Coming Shiny — Map existing Shiny knowledge RDesk mental model. Cookbook — Common recipes file dialogs, charts, native menus. Async Guide — Deep dive background task management.

title: RDesk IPC Message Contract
text: document defines standard JSON envelope used communication R backend HTML/JS frontend RDesk applications.

title: RDesk IPC Message Contract
text: Every message sent across native IPC bridge (WebView2 stdin/stdout) must JSON object following structure:

previous_headings: RDesk IPC Message Contract (v1.0) > The Message Envelope
title: RDesk IPC Message Contract
text: id: unique string identifier message (e.g., tracing request/response correlation). type: internal name message action. triggers handlers receiving end. version: version message contract (currently “1.0”). payload: JSON object containing actual data message. timestamp: Seconds since Unix epoch (including decimal fractions).

title: RDesk IPC Message Contract
text: sending R UI, use app$send(): RDesk automatically wraps payload standard envelope transmission.

title: RDesk IPC Message Contract
text: sending UI R, use rdesk.send(): rdesk.js library automatically constructs envelope unique ID current timestamp.

title: RDesk IPC Message Contract
text: parser rdesk_parse_message() designed defensive. return NULL : 1. input valid JSON. 2. envelope missing required fields (type payload). cases, warning() emitted specific failure reason.

previous_headings: RDesk IPC Message Contract (v1.0) > Error Handling
title: RDesk IPC Message Contract
text: building custom extensions handlers, always check NULL processing:
code: msg <- rdesk_parse_message(raw_json) if (is.null(msg)) {   # Log error or silently ignore malformed traffic   return(invisible(NULL)) }  # Safely access msg$type and msg$payload here

title: RDesk IPC Message Contract
text: using async(), result automatically sent back UI message type <original_type>_result. example: convention means JS developer always knows event listen without reading R code. consistent predictable.

previous_headings: RDesk IPC Message Contract (v1.0) > The async result convention
title: RDesk IPC Message Contract
text: need different result type, use rdesk_async() directly (Tier 2) call app$send() explicitly on_done callback.

title: Coming from Shiny
text: Shiny uses reactive graph – inputs change, outputs update automatically. RDesk uses explicit message passing – UI sends message, R handles pushes result back. feels like writing API handler Shiny server.

title: Coming from Shiny
text: Shiny RDesk key difference: RDesk re-run automatically. UI explicitly calls rdesk.send("filter", {cyl: 6}) receives filter_result back.
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$plot <- renderPlot({     filtered <- mtcars[mtcars$cyl == input$cyl, ]     plot(filtered$wt, filtered$mpg)   }) } app$on_message("filter", async(function(payload) {   filtered <- mtcars[mtcars$cyl == payload$cyl, ]   list(chart = rdesk_plot_to_base64(     plot(filtered$wt, filtered$mpg)   )) }, app = app))

title: Coming from Shiny
text: Shiny RDesk
code: output$table <- renderTable({ mtcars }) # In R -- push data explicitly app$send("table_data", list(   rows = lapply(seq_len(nrow(mtcars)), function(i) as.list(mtcars[i,])),   cols = names(mtcars) )) // In JavaScript -- receive it rdesk.on("table_data", function(data) {   renderTable(data.rows, data.cols); });

title: Coming from Shiny
text: Shiny RDesk
code: output$download <- downloadHandler(   filename = "data.csv",   content  = function(file) write.csv(mtcars, file) ) app$on_message("save_csv", function(payload) {   path <- app$dialog_save(     title   = "Save CSV",     filters = "CSV files (*.csv)|*.csv"   )   if (!is.null(path)) write.csv(mtcars, path, row.names = FALSE) })

title: Coming from Shiny
code: app$set_menu(list(   File = list(     "Open..."  = function() app$dialog_open(),     "Save..."  = function() app$dialog_save(),     "---",     "Exit"     = app$quit   ),   Help = list(     "About"    = function() app$toast("MyApp v1.0", type = "info")   ) ))

title: Coming from Shiny
text: Shiny (future/promises) RDesk RDesk’s async() handles loading overlay, cancellation, result routing automatically.
code: library(future) plan(multisession)  observeEvent(input$run, {   future({     slow_model(input$data)   }) %...>% (function(result) {     output$result <- renderText(result)   }) }) app$on_message("run_model", async(function(payload) {   slow_model(payload$data) }, app = app, loading_message = "Running model..."))

title: Coming from Shiny
text: Everything touch Shiny inputs/outputs moves across directly: data loading transformation code ggplot2 chart code (render rdesk_plot_to_base64()) statistical modelling code file reading writing code helper functions

title: Coming from Shiny
text: Keep data modelling R files unchanged Replace ui.R www/index.html (plain HTML – Shiny DSL) Replace server.R handlers one one using app$on_message() Replace renderPlot calls rdesk_plot_to_base64() + app$send() Run source("app.R") test handler migrate

title: Contributing to RDesk
text: develop RDesk folder synced OneDrive similar. g++ compiler reads stale cached source files synced folders, producing ghost bugs code changes effect. Always work local non-synced directory C:/Projects/RDesk. build system copies source temp directory mitigate , working locally still strongly recommended.

title: Contributing to RDesk
Contributing to RDesk
text: messages R JavaScript use standard envelope: {id, type, version, payload, timestamp}. See vignettes/ipc-contract.Rmd full specification. Never break contract without bumping version zzz.R.

title: Contributing to RDesk
text: One feature fix per PR R code must pass devtools::check() zero warnings C++ changes must compile cleanly Rtools44 g++ Update NEWS.md change Update relevant vignettes public API changes

what: RDesk - Native Desktop Apps for R
title: RDesk - Native Desktop Apps for R
text: RDesk framework building native Windows desktop applications R. turns logic standalone .exe runs machine—R installation required end-user.

title: RDesk - Native Desktop Apps for R
text: Get professional dashboard running seconds:
code: # 1. Install RDesk devtools::install_github("Janakiraman-311/RDesk")  # 2. Create a working dashboard RDesk::rdesk_create_app("MyDashboard")

title: RDesk - Native Desktop Apps for R
text: RDesk solves “Last Mile” problem R deployment. Instead browser URL, give users familiar Windows tool.

title: RDesk - Native Desktop Apps for R
text: Data analysts building internal tools live server Consultants distributing one-analysis tools clients Teams replacing Excel macros proper R-powered apps Organisations need offline, zero--involvement deployment right fit web applications, cross-platform needs, real-time collaborative tools — use Shiny .

title: RDesk - Native Desktop Apps for R
text: 🔒 Zero-Port IPC: Native bidirectional pipes R UI. firewall issues port conflicts. ⚡ Async Default: Built-background task processing via mirai. UI never freezes, even heavy R computations. 📦 Portable Runtime: Packages minimal R distribution .exe. users don’t need install R. 🎨 Modern Web UI: Use HTML/JS/CSS interface keeping 100% logic R. 🛠 Professional Scaffolding: Generate dashboards sidebar navigation, Dark Mode, auto-wired charts one command.

title: RDesk - Native Desktop Apps for R
text: Building professional installer single command away:
code: RDesk::build_app(   app_dir         = "MyDashboard",   app_name        = "SalesTool",   build_installer = TRUE ) # Output: dist/SalesTool-1.0.0-setup.exe

title: RDesk - Native Desktop Apps for R
text: Visit full documentation janakiraman-311.github.io/RDesk Getting Started — zero first native app. Coming Shiny — side--side guide mapping Shiny knowledge RDesk. Async Guide — Mastering background tasks progress overlays. Cookbook — Copy-paste recipes common desktop patterns.

code: # Safe logical check (unwrapped) app_dir <- system.file("templates/hello", package = "RDesk") if (nzchar(app_dir)) {   message("Built-in app directory: ", app_dir) } #> Built-in app directory: C:/Users/runneradmin/AppData/Local/R/cache/R/renv/library/RDesk-47d971e3/windows/R-4.5/x86_64-w64-mingw32/RDesk/templates/hello  if (interactive()) {   app <- App$new(title = "Car Visualizer", width = 1200, height = 800)      app$on_ready(function() {     message("App is ready!")   })      # Handle messages from UI   app$on_message("get_data", function(payload) {     list(cars = mtcars[1:5, ])   })      # Start the app   app$run() }

what: Wrap a message handler to run asynchronously with zero configuration — async
title: Wrap a message handler to run asynchronously with zero configuration — async
text: async() simplest way make RDesk message handler non-blocking. Wrap handler function async() RDesk automatically handles background execution, loading states, error toasts, result routing. async() transforms standard RDesk message handler background task. UI remains responsive task runs. finished, result message (e.g., get_data_result) automatically sent back UI.

title: Wrap a message handler to run asynchronously with zero configuration — async
code: async(   fn,   app = NULL,   loading_message = "Working...",   cancellable = TRUE,   error_message = "Error: " )

title: Wrap a message handler to run asynchronously with zero configuration — async
text: fn handler function, taking payload argument. app RDesk App instance. NULL, tries resolve global registry. loading_message Message display UI overlay working. cancellable Whether UI show 'Cancel' button. error_message Prefix toast notifications task fails.

title: Wrap a message handler to run asynchronously with zero configuration — async
text: wrapped handler function suitable app$on_message().

title: Wrap a message handler to run asynchronously with zero configuration — async
text: ensure background worker access application logic, RDesk automatically sources every .R file application's R/ directory executing task. also snapshots currently loaded packages (excluding system packages) recreate environment.

title: Wrap a message handler to run asynchronously with zero configuration — async
code: if (interactive()) {   app$on_message("filter_cars", async(function(payload) {     mtcars[mtcars$cyl == payload$cylinders, ]   }, app = app)) }

what: Build a self-contained distributable from an RDesk application — build_app
title: Build a self-contained distributable from an RDesk application — build_app
text: Build self-contained distributable RDesk application

title: Build a self-contained distributable from an RDesk application — build_app
code: build_app(   app_dir = ".",   out_dir = file.path(tempdir(), "dist"),   app_name = NULL,   version = NULL,   r_version = NULL,   include_packages = character(0),   portable_r_method = c("extract_only", "installer"),   runtime_dir = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   build_installer = FALSE,   publisher = "RDesk User",   website = "https://github.com/Janakiraman-311/RDesk",   license_file = NULL,   icon_file = NULL,   prune_runtime = TRUE,   dry_run = FALSE )

title: Build a self-contained distributable from an RDesk application — build_app
text: app_dir Path app directory (must contain app.R www/) out_dir Output directory zip file (created exists) app_name Name application. Defaults name DESCRIPTION "MyRDeskApp". version Version string. Defaults version DESCRIPTION "1.0.0". r_version R version bundle e.g. "4.4.2". Defaults current R version. include_packages Character vector extra CRAN packages bundle. RDesk's dependencies always included automatically. portable_r_method provision bundled R runtime runtime_dir supplied. "extract_only" requires standalone 7-Zip never launches R installer. "installer" allows legacy silent installer path explicitly. runtime_dir Optional path existing portable R runtime root containing bin/. supplied, RDesk copies runtime directly skips download/extract step. overwrite TRUE, overwrite existing output. Default FALSE. build_installer TRUE, also build Windows installer (.exe) using InnoSetup. publisher Documentation application publisher (used installer). website URL application website (used installer). license_file Path license file (.txt .rtf) include installer. icon_file Path .ico file installer application shortcut. prune_runtime TRUE, remove unnecessary files (Tcl/Tk, docs, tests) bundled R runtime reduce size (~15-20MB saving). Default TRUE. dry_run TRUE, performs quick validation app structure environment without performing full build. Default FALSE.

title: Build a self-contained distributable from an RDesk application — build_app
text: Path created zip file, invisibly.

title: Build a self-contained distributable from an RDesk application — build_app
code: # Prepare an app directory (following scaffold example) app_path <- file.path(tempdir(), "MyApp") rdesk_create_app("MyApp", path = tempdir()) #>  #> [RDesk] Generating MyApp... #> [RDesk]   Created 6 directories and 8 files #>  #> [RDesk] Successfully created: C:\Users\RUNNER~1\AppData\Local\Temp\RtmpUnpue2\MyApp #> [RDesk] Your Professional Dashboard includes: #>   - Mixed visualization (Charts + Tables) #>   - Built-in KPI cards system #>   - Sidebar filtering engine #>   - Background processing (Async Workers) #>  #> [RDesk] Run it now: #>   setwd("C:\Users\runneradmin\AppData\Local\Temp\RtmpUnpue2\MyApp") #>   source("app.R") #>  #> [RDesk] Build your executable when ready: #>   RDesk::build_app(app_dir = "C:\Users\runneradmin\AppData\Local\Temp\RtmpUnpue2\MyApp", app_name = "MyApp") #>   # Perform a dry-run build (fast, no external binaries downloaded) build_app(app_path, out_dir = tempdir(), dry_run = TRUE) #>  #> [RDesk] DRY RUN: Validating app structure... #> [RDesk]   V Structure OK #> [RDesk]   V RTools found: C:\rtools45 #> [RDesk] DRY RUN: All checks passed.

title: Run a task in the background — rdesk_async
text: task function run background. args list arguments pass task. on_done Callback function(result) called task finishes successfully. on_error Callback function(error) called task fails. timeout_sec Optional timeout seconds. exceeded, job cancelled on_error() receives timeout error. app_id Optional App ID used associate job specific app.

title: Run a task in the background — rdesk_async
text: Invisible job ID.

title: Run a task in the background — rdesk_async
code: # Fast, non-interactive task check (safe to unwrap) rdesk_jobs_pending() #> [1] 0  if (interactive()) {   # Run a long-running computation in the background   rdesk_async(     task = function(n) { Sys.sleep(2); sum(runif(n)) },     args = list(n = 1e6),     on_done = function(res) message("Task finished: ", res),     on_error = function(err) message("Task failed: ", err$message)   ) }

title: RDesk 1.0.0 (2026-03-22)
text: Native Window Management: Added app$set_size(), app$set_position(), app$minimize(), app$maximize(), app$restore(), app$fullscreen(), app$always_on_top(). Enhanced Native Dialogs: Implemented app$dialog_folder(), app$message_box() (OK/Yes//Cancel icons), app$dialog_color(). Recursive Menus: Native Win32 menu bars now support arbitrarily deep nesting checkable items. System Tray Context Menus: Added app$set_tray_menu() native right-click interaction. Global Hotkeys: Added app$register_hotkey() system-wide keyboard shortcuts. Clipboard: Added app$clipboard_read() app$clipboard_write(). Anti-Zombie Watchdog: Native launcher now auto-terminates parent R process dies. Single-Instance Lock: Prevents running multiple copies application. Close Interception: Added app$on_close() intercept cancel window exit attempts. Persistent Logging: Success error logs now written %LOCALAPPDATA%/RDesk. Auto-Updater: Added app$check_update() detect link remote application updates. renv integration: RDesk development environment built bundles now lockable via renv.lock reproducible distribution.

title: RDesk 1.0.0 (2026-03-22)
text: Fixed COM reference count leaks Unicode path handling native launcher. Resolved R6 namespace collisions complex application initialization. Hardened build_app(dry_run = TRUE) rapid environment validation. Fixed plot rendering synchronization background worker pools.

title: RDesk 0.9.0 (2026-03-19)
text: Removed httpuv dependency entirely. Apps built earlier versions must rebuilt new launcher.

title: RDesk 0.9.0 (2026-03-19)
text: Zero-port native IPC architecture using WebView2 PostWebMessageAsString stdin/stdout pipe. Virtual hostname mapping via SetVirtualHostNameToFolderMapping – app assets load disk HTTP server. Three-tier async engine: async() wrapper, rdesk_async(), direct mirai access. 5.9x faster task startup vs callr alone. Loading overlay system progress bar, cancellation, toast notifications built framework. build_app() now supports build_installer = TRUE InnoSetup Windows installer generation. rdesk_create_app() scaffold generates complete working app structure ready run. GitHub Actions CI/CD three workflows: R-CMD-check, build-app, release. Comprehensive error logging – crash.log rdesk_startup.log written failure. Native Windows popup crash. System tray, native menus, file dialogs, toast notifications.

title: RDesk 0.9.0 (2026-03-19)
text: Fixed bundled dialog, tray, notify, IPC command handling App.R packaged apps longer try write null window process handle. Hardened launcher shutdown, notification handling, request IDs, menu/tray updates, save-dialog Unicode handling across native C++ launcher window.R. Fixed bundled stub logging stdout handling packaged apps preserve IPC still writing crash diagnostics. Fixed scaffolded apps demo apps bundled mode longer sinks stdout, app paths resolve reliably, generated tests/plots use safer defaults. Fixed build_app() package bundling, temp cleanup, empty-directory copies, added explicit portable-R strategies via portable_r_method runtime_dir. Fixed GitHub Actions bundled-app workflows use explicit installer runtime provisioning current setup actions. Fixed COM reference count leak C++ launcher shutdown. Replaced hardcoded personal paths dynamic environment variable lookups build.R. Fixed duplicate publisher parameter build_app() signature. Corrected R-CMD-check YAML quoting args error-fields. Removed dead httpuv private fields App R6 class. Fixed @export tags incorrectly placed private R6 methods. Unicode path handling hardened using MultiByteToWideChar throughout C++ launcher.

title: RDesk 0.9.0 (2026-03-19)
text: IPC message envelope standardised id, type, version, payload, timestamp fields across R JavaScript. Version centralised via getOption(“rdesk.ipc_version”). CI guard added – headless environments skip native window init. mirai daemon pool pre-warmed Apprun()andshutdowncleanlyatApprun() shut cleanly Appcleanup().
